summary: 7 findings documented across 7 vulnerability categories. Each finding has a vulnerable endpoint, a secure counterpart, evidence, and remediation. All tests run against an in-process lab application.

findings.title: SQL Injection in Search Endpoint
findings.vulnerable_behavior: User input interpolated into SQL query via f-string. Attacker can modify query logic, dump tables, or bypass filters.
findings.secure_behavior: Parameterized query with ? placeholder. Input is never interpreted as SQL.
evidence: Payload ' UNION SELECT id,username,password_hash FROM users -- dumps credentials
findings.impact: Full database read access; potential data exfiltration of all notes and user credentials.
findings.remediation: Use parameterized queries: conn.execute('... WHERE title LIKE ?', (f'%{q}%',))

findings.title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) on Note Access
findings.vulnerable_behavior: user_id taken from query parameter, not from authenticated token. Any caller can read any note by supplying any user_id.
findings.secure_behavior: user_id extracted from validated JWT. Query enforces AND user_id = ? ownership check.
evidence: GET /vulnerable/notes/3?user_id=1 returns bob's note as alice
No token required on vulnerable endpoint
findings.impact: Any authenticated or unauthenticated user can read all notes in the database.
findings.remediation: Extract user identity from JWT payload, never from request parameters. Enforce ownership with AND user_id = ? in query.

findings.title: Weak JWT — Hardcoded Secret and No Expiry Enforcement
findings.vulnerable_behavior: Token signed with hardcoded string 'secret'. Expiry verification disabled. Attacker can forge tokens or replay stale tokens.
findings.secure_behavior: Token signed with long random secret from config. Short expiry (30 min) enforced on every request.
evidence: options={'verify_exp': False} in decode — expiry never checked
Forged token with any payload accepted by /vulnerable/me
findings.impact: Authentication bypass. Attacker can impersonate any user by forging a token.
findings.remediation: Generate secret with secrets.token_hex(32). Always include exp claim and let jose enforce it by default.

findings.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Fetch Endpoint
findings.vulnerable_behavior: httpx.get(url) called without any host or scheme validation. Attacker can reach internal services, metadata endpoints, or loopback interfaces.
findings.secure_behavior: URL validated before request: scheme must be http/https, resolved IP must not be private, loopback, or link-local.
findings.impact: Internal network scanning, cloud metadata access (AWS IMDSv1), or reading local files via file:// scheme.
findings.remediation: Resolve hostname to IP, check against RFC1918/loopback/link-local ranges. Allowlist expected external hostnames when possible.

findings.title: Stored Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in Comments
findings.vulnerable_behavior:
User input stored as raw HTML: f'

{content}

'. Script tags and event handlers rendered in the browser.
findings.secure_behavior: html.escape(content) applied before constructing HTML. CSP header added to rendered response.
findings.impact: Session hijacking, credential theft, malicious redirects for all users who view the comments page.
findings.remediation: Always escape user content with html.escape() before inserting into HTML. Add Content-Security-Policy header. Consider a templating engine with auto-escaping.

findings.title: Insecure File Upload — Missing Extension Check and Path Traversal
findings.vulnerable_behavior: No file extension validation. Original filename used for stored path. Attacker can upload .php files or use path traversal (../../etc/passwd).
findings.secure_behavior: Extension whitelist enforced. Stored filename is a UUID. Size limit checked. No original filename used in filesystem path.
evidence: shell.php accepted and stored at upload_dir/shell.php
../../etc/passwd used as stored path on vulnerable endpoint
findings.impact: Remote code execution if web server executes uploaded scripts. Overwrite of arbitrary files via path traversal.
findings.remediation: Enforce extension whitelist. Generate UUID-based stored name. Check file size. Consider validating magic bytes, not just extension.

findings.title: Security Misconfiguration — Debug Endpoint Exposes Secrets
findings.vulnerable_behavior: Debug endpoint returns all environment variables, JWT secret, DB path, and Python executable path in JSON response.
evidence: Environment variables including PATH and PYTHONPATH exposed
findings.impact: Full secret disclosure. Attacker gains JWT signing key, enabling auth bypass. Internal config reveals attack surface.
findings.remediation: Remove debug endpoints before any deployment. Never expose env vars or secrets via API. Use structured logging to safe sinks.

limitations: All vulnerabilities are demonstrated against synthetic in-process data only.
SSRF checks test URL validation logic — no actual outbound requests to external hosts.
File upload tests use in-memory uploads; no real malicious code is executed.
Detection patterns in sast_checks.py are heuristic and may produce false positives.
This is a controlled lab, not a comprehensive scanner or a production security tool.
